name: How can I tell if my bathroom has mold or water damage before remodeling?
headline: How can I tell if my bathroom has mold or water damage before remodeling?

mainEntity.name: How can I tell if my bathroom has mold or water damage before remodeling?
mainEntity.text: How can I tell if my bathroom has mold or water damage before remodeling?

acceptedAnswer.text: To know if your bathroom has mold or water damage before a remodel, start by looking, smelling, and feeling. Dark spots on drywall, soft or spongy flooring near the tub or toilet, loose or cracked tiles, peeling paint, or a musty smell are all warning signs. Stains on ceilings below the bathroom can also point to slow leaks. In older coastal homes around Pacifica, Daly City, and Half Moon Bay, moisture buildup from fog and poor ventilation is common. If your fan is weak, noisy, or vents into the attic instead of outside, the risk of hidden damage is higher. Before demo, you can: - Check around the tub, shower curb, and toilet base for softness - Look inside access panels (if any) for water stains - Ask a plumber or contractor to inspect visible pipes and connections Once walls and floors are opened, your contractor should check for rot in studs, subfloor, and framing. Make sure your contract explains how hidden damage will be handled and priced if it’s found. A major pitfall is ignoring early signs because “it looks okay on the surface.” Hidden damage doesn’t go away and is cheaper to fix while the bathroom is already open. Plan some extra money and time for potential repairs so you’re not forced to cut corners on waterproofing or ventilation when problems appear.
